findings.description: Pagination offset uses page*page_size instead of (page-1)*page_size, so 1-based page 1 skips the first page and the first page_size results are unreachable.
User-supplied name_pattern is compiled and run as a regex, enabling ReDoS (catastrophic backtracking) that can hang the request thread.
re.compile on an invalid user-supplied name_pattern raises re.error which is uncaught and surfaces as an unhandled 500.
Fetches all matching rows then filters/sorts/paginates in Python instead of using SQL LIMIT/OFFSET, loading the entire result set into memory.
page_size is only upper-bounded; zero or negative page_size and non-positive page are not validated, yielding empty or wrong windows.
With a negative computed offset, Python slicing wraps from the end and silently returns unexpected rows instead of an empty page.
matcher.search(u['display_name']) raises TypeError if display_name is NULL/None for any row.
departments() can include None in the returned list if the department column is nullable, violating the list[str] annotation.
Repeated open/try-finally/close boilerplate across methods could use a context-manager helper for the connection.
